article_title: Minnesota Senate Passes HF 1606 65-0, Sending Civil Ban on AI 'Nudification' Apps to Gov. Tim Walz

content_preview.title: Minnesota Senate Passes HF 1606 65-0, Sending Civil Ban on AI 'Nudification' Apps to Gov. Tim Walz
content_preview.summary: Minnesota's HF 1606 cleared the state Senate 65-0 after a 132-1 House vote, sending an AI 'nudification' app ban to Gov. Tim Walz with $500,000 civil penalties and a private right of action.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The Minnesota Senate has passed House File 1606, a bill that bars websites and apps from offering AI tools that generate realistic fake nude images of identifiable people without their consent. The Senate cleared the bill 65-0 on Thursday and sent it to Gov. Tim Walz for signature, [Decrypt reported](https://decrypt.co/366388/minnesota-moves-ban-ai-apps-generate-fake-nude-images). The state House had already approved the same measure 132-1 on April 23, [CBS Minnesota's WCCO Staff re...
